unexpected_finds.description: AMS Math of Computation has used double-anonymous peer review since January 2021. This was not in the prior session's working assumption set (Stage-2 enumeration treated MComp as standard single-anonymous). This is materially positive for the pseudonymous-author posture because referees do not see author identity during review, which structurally isolates this submission from the 8 prior Experimental Mathematics rejections.
unexpected_finds.impact: Strengthens MComp as primary recommendation; should be reflected in the cover letter framing (no need to address the rejection history).

unexpected_finds.description: Cambridge University Press has an explicit policy clause about pseudonymous authorship: 'Journals may have their own policies on anonymous or pseudonymous authorship.' This is the only top-5 venue whose ethics chain explicitly anticipates the pseudonymous-author case. For the broader portfolio (which uses 'Shunsuke Kubota' with ORCID 0009-0000-6192-8273 without institutional affiliation), CUP-published journals are structurally easier to work with than venues whose authorship policies are silent on pseudonymity.
unexpected_finds.impact: Elevates Bulletin of the Australian Mathematical Society from 'general fast-decision option' to 'pseudonymity-policy-aware fast-decision option' and suggests CUP-published journals as a portfolio-wide preference axis.
